summary: The H/V system separates main-signal authority (Hp) from advance warning at a distant signal (Vr).

mainAspects.title: Stop
mainAspects.recognition: One red light, or on some combined station signals two red lights horizontally.
playerAction: Do not pass without an appropriate authority or subsidiary indication.

pzb.summary: Passing an active 2000 Hz magnet without authority causes an emergency brake application.

mainAspects.title: Proceed
mainAspects.meaning: Proceed at the speed permitted by the timetable and any additional speed indication.
playerAction: Observe any Zs 3 speed indicator or other restriction.

pzb.summary: No acknowledgement is normally required for the plain proceed aspect.

mainAspects.title: Proceed slowly
mainAspects.meaning: Proceed at reduced speed. Unless another speed is indicated, the H/V slow-speed rule applies from the signal through the relevant points area.
playerAction: Reduce to the applicable speed by the signal.
Check for a Zs 3 indicator that replaces the default speed value.

pzb.summary: PZB response depends on the applicable speed indication and active magnet arrangement.

distantAspects.title: Expect stop
distantAspects.recognition: Two steady yellow lights rising from left to right.
distantAspects.meaning: Expect the associated main signal to show stop.
playerAction: Acknowledge PZB when the 1000 Hz influence is active.
Brake so you can stop before the associated main signal.

distantAspects.title: Expect proceed
distantAspects.recognition: Two steady green lights rising from left to right.
distantAspects.meaning: Expect the associated main signal to show proceed.
playerAction: Continue at the permitted speed unless another restriction applies.

distantAspects.title: Expect proceed slowly
distantAspects.meaning: Expect the associated main signal to show Hp 2 or another reduced-speed indication.
playerAction: Begin braking for the reduced speed at the main signal.
Check any Zs 3v announcement for the exact speed.
